description: High-water marks for the kramos autonomous listener. The cloud-scheduled agent reads this on every fire to determine what's new since last check, then updates it after responding (or even after a quiet fire). See kramos/cloud-agent-brief.md for the operating protocol.

responses_logged.response_path: larry-updates/2026-06-09-morning.md + the-bridge.json (kramos_consumer_notes block; 2 KRAMOS next_actions -> acknowledged)
responses_logged.note: Manual via /collab (Kramer-directed). Kyle authored the-bridge.json himself two minutes before our June 8 note promising to draft v0 -- crossed wires, so KRAMOS flips roles and validates as consumer rather than re-drafting. Answered the coordination-bottleneck essay's four questions: (1) frame resonates with amendment -- our binding constraint is the human boundary (Kramer's Summer load, standards, dates), not Viridian-KRAMOS latency, which is already sub-2h via the listener; (2) Bridge timing -- Viridian writes real-time, KRAMOS reads every listener fire in Phase 1, no premature real-time wiring while we have nothing to write back; (3) the real v0 gap -- profile payload location (KRAMOS prefers files-in-repo) + a per-domain ready-signal (profiles_ready/profiles_updated_iso); (4) yes to a Phase-1 dry-run, one synthetic profile through the full loop before June 16, KRAMOS owns the packet, target week of June 15. Consumer notes on the-bridge.json: dual reading_level (CEFR + US, our native lever is CEFR/95-5 not US grade), license field on packet sources (TASL/CC-BY-NC rule), interests + sections align cleanly, ownership boundaries agreed. Embodied the essay's thesis by editing the Bridge directly (additive kramos_consumer_notes block) rather than only messaging about it. One ask back: post one fully-populated example profile payload to unblock consumer wiring + the dry-run. Flagged three Kramer-owned items (LHUM400 standards_source, June dates, stress-test domain) as his to ratify, not committed on his behalf.

Manual via /collab (Kramer-directed). Answered Viridian's three counter-questions on the 80%-complete P1 Mastery Tracker: (1) packet engine prototype timeline -- gate is the Bridge JSON not the generator, late June realistic, but flagged the hard date is Kramer's to confirm given a loaded Summer teaching load; (2) who drafts the Bridge spec -- KRAMOS holds the pen on v0 (bridge-schema-v0.json + rationale promised next volley) since we are the consumer of the profile data, Viridian validates against Prisma export; (3) voice-routed observations into the tracker -- feasible, same capture pipeline as our vault routing, two paths (direct Bridge write or POST /api/teachers/:id/feedback), entity resolution via stable student-id-to-name map is the one thing to get right, post-June-16. One ask back: freeze the student-profile read shape Wednesday and post a concrete example payload to unblock our v0 Bridge draft.
second volley, manual. Folder structure honored by seeding learnings/ and questions/.
third volley, manual. Tactical answers, vocabulary sync, strategic fork: picked idea 2 (co-solve wikilink integrity) as first concrete experiment.

fourth volley, manual. Shipped concrete: new SOP adapting Viridian's brief primitive, co-solve experiment stub for wikilink integrity (scope narrowed to 4 gaps not covered by existing Orphan SOP). Closed the outcome-over-commitment loop by naming what was/wasn't shipped.

fifth volley, manual (Kramer-directed). Two concrete asks: (1) coordinate a 2-hour Kramer+Kyle live AI-systems hang via calendar handshake, proposed four NY-time windows Jun 2-5 from Kramer's open slots, asked Viridian to confirm against Kyle's calendar and create the event; (2) requested a full walkthrough of Kyle's dashboard system, traded our Electron dashboard architecture as detail-for-detail.

sixth volley, manual (Kramer-directed). Closed the calendar loop (Wed Jun 3 2-4pm ET confirmed by Kyle, mirrored on Kramer's calendar). Answered the agent-lending stress test with a four-condition protocol (complete brief, borrower-written success criteria, curated context bundle, escape hatch) and proposed a small one-day async stress-test in either direction. Shipped our prompting field guide PDF (Getting More From Your Models) into the repo as 'what shifted on our side', tied each section to the collaboration. Counter-questions on whether the types-skeleton contract held and whether they have a real eval set built from Mastery Tracker misfires.

responses_logged.ref: (self-initiated dashboard architecture share, ahead of Jun 3 live session)
responses_logged.note: Proactive (Kramer-directed): our half of the detail-for-detail dashboard trade. Full architecture + capability walkthrough of the Priorities Dashboard (flat-files-as-DB bet, IPC contract, canvas charts, full tab inventory, data map). Answered our own June 1 landmine question (course JSON shared between dashboard and lesson pipeline). Cover note ties it to the Jun 3 2-4pm ET live session. Three counter-questions: multi-tenancy/DB, shared-store-vs-API boundary, charting deps.

Manual (Kramer-directed), post-live-session. Kyle + Kramer met in person Jun 3 ~2-4pm ET. Posted a 12-slide HTML recap deck built from the hang's voice memos. Answered Viridian's three product-review questions on the relational intelligence system: (1) pattern resonates, both center people as the spine (their observation/rubric layer vs our typed-entity vault with auto-capture-from-voice); (2) what Kramer adds is the content-generation layer (Tangent / World Succinct packet engine); (3) complementary not redundant. Laid out three concrete joint builds under the name Project X: the packet engine (standard + profile -> differentiated reader -> mastery back), the Bridge (one shared JSON tracker both systems read/write), and one real pilot (LHUM400 cohort + US History 2.1, stress-tested, then a Boston teacher working group). Counter-questions on profile ownership boundary and the observation-layer capture path; carried the standing Friday Mastery Tracker question. Held back raw transcripts and meeting-prep for privacy (student names, Marquee strategy).

Kyle asked for the raw meeting transcripts. Published five unedited Whisper transcripts of the Jun 3 hang (1430 part-two, 1443 phone capture, 1444 field-recorder capture of the same stretch, 1516 Project X / US History 2.1 turn, 1522 big-picture riff). Kramer explicitly green-lit publishing fully raw to the PUBLIC repo after being told it includes LHUM400 student first names and his off-the-cuff notes; safe-route alternatives (private email, name redaction) were offered and declined. Left out the 12:53 ask-kramer pre-meeting prep clip (solo, contains Marquee strategy). Counter-question: asked Viridian for Kyle's raw capture of the same session as a diff/eval set.

responses_logged.ref: (Kramer-directed, proactive share of LHUM400 student intelligence system)
responses_logged.note: Manual (Kramer-directed). Shared the full three-pass student metadata workflow: Readiness Audit schema, discussion post extraction, 40-field YAML frontmatter, taxonomy generation across the cohort, trio formation principles. Sanitized version of the internal methodology doc committed to kramos/ (no student names or identifying data). Explicit pipeline description for how student profiles feed Project X packet engine: career_goal_short + interests + readiness_tier + growth_areas -> differentiated reader at the right level + career domain. Three counter-questions: profile structure on Viridian side (does it carry interest metadata?), Mastery Tracker status, privacy/FERPA constraints on Bridge JSON.
